headline: The Long Collapse Century: September 11 Attacks

events.title: Opioid Crisis
events.description: Purdue Pharma criminally prosecuted in 2004
events.narration: The aggressive federal prosecution of Purdue Pharma in 2004, launched by Attorney General John Ashcroft following mounting evidence of deliberate misrepresentation, marked a decisive break from the regulatory capture that had enabled the opioid epidemic's early expansion. Unlike the civil settlements and deferred prosecution agreements that would characterize enforcement in other timelines, the criminal convictions of three Purdue executives—including Richard Sackler—sent immediate shockwaves through the pharmaceutical industry. This early intervention, combined with the FDA's subsequent moratorium on new extended-release opioid approvals, contained what epidemiologists now recognize as a crisis that claimed over 500,000 lives in parallel histories. The Sackler family's forced divestiture of their pharmaceutical holdings in 2005 became a template for corporate accountability that would reshape American business regulation for the remainder of the century.

events.title: Social Media Emergence
events.description: Ad-funded, engagement-maximizing platforms dominate

events.title: Drone Warfare Program
events.description: Drone technology proliferates to 30+ nations
events.narration: The targeted nature of America's post-2001 military response, focused primarily on intelligence operations rather than large-scale ground invasions, paradoxically accelerated the global spread of drone technology as nations sought asymmetric capabilities without the massive logistical footprint of traditional warfare. By mid-2004, Israeli, Chinese, and European defense contractors had successfully reverse-engineered and improved upon early Predator designs, while Pakistan, Iran, and Turkey launched indigenous programs that would mature within the decade. This rapid proliferation occurred three to four years earlier than in our baseline timeline, driven by the demonstrated effectiveness of precision strikes combined with the absence of major conventional conflicts that might have otherwise absorbed military R&D resources. The result was a fundamentally altered strategic landscape by 2008, with over thirty nations possessing operational unmanned combat aerial vehicles rather than the handful that existed in our original timeline.

events.title: Record Hurricane Season
events.description: Four major hurricanes hit Florida; FEMA weaknesses exposed

events.title: Indian Ocean Tsunami
events.description: 230K dead across 14 countries; no warning system existed
events.narration: The Indian Ocean tsunami of December 26, 2004, struck with the same devastating force as in our original timeline, claiming 230,000 lives across fourteen nations from Indonesia to Somalia. The absence of any regional early warning system meant that coastal communities faced the waves with no preparation, despite hours passing between the initial earthquake off Sumatra and the tsunami's arrival at distant shores. This natural catastrophe would prove to be one of the few events of the decade that unfolded identically across both timelines, a reminder that even in our divergent history, the earth's geological forces remained unchanged. The international humanitarian response that followed helped solidify the emerging multilateral frameworks that had been strengthened by the previous year's diplomatic resolution of the Iraq crisis.

events.title: Hurricane Katrina Response
events.description: FEMA acts within 24 hours; ~400 deaths
events.explanation: Pre-positioned assets and faster evacuation

events.title: North Korea Nuclear
events.description: Kim regime collapses; chaotic transition
events.narration: The Kim Jong-il regime's sudden collapse in October 2006 followed mounting internal pressure from military factions emboldened by widespread access to drone surveillance technology that had exposed the leadership's vulnerabilities. Unlike our timeline where North Korea would successfully test nuclear weapons and maintain dynastic rule for decades, the chaotic power vacuum left China scrambling to manage millions of refugees streaming across the Yalu River while South Korea found itself unprepared for the logistical nightmare of potential reunification. The regime's nuclear program, still in its nascent stages, was abandoned amid the political upheaval, though several key scientists defected to Iran with critical technical knowledge. By November, a provisional military council had assumed control in Pyongyang, but effective governance remained elusive as competing factions vied for legitimacy.

events.title: Haiti Earthquake
events.description: Massive earthquake devastates Haiti; international response widely criticized
events.narration: The January 12th earthquake that struck Haiti proceeded along familiar lines, with the 7.0 magnitude tremor killing over 200,000 and displacing more than a million in Port-au-Prince and surrounding areas. The international response, led by the United Nations and various NGOs, drew the same criticisms that had marked such efforts in our timeline—coordination failures, cultural insensitivity, and the introduction of cholera by peacekeeping forces. What differed was the technological landscape of relief efforts: without dominant social media platforms to mobilize instant global awareness and crowdsourced aid, traditional media and established humanitarian organizations bore the full weight of response coordination. The disaster unfolded as a more contained tragedy in the global consciousness, receiving sustained but less immediate international attention than similar catastrophes would garner in other timelines.

events.title: Arab Spring
events.description: Tunisia succeeds; Egypt coup; Libya/Syria civil war
events.narration: The self-immolation of Mohamed Bouazizi in Sidi Bouzid on December 17, 2010, ignited protests that would topple Ben Ali's government within weeks, marking the beginning of what became known as the Arab Spring. As in our timeline's history, Tunisia's relatively peaceful transition would prove the exception rather than the rule, with Egypt's brief democratic experiment ending in military coup by 2013, while Libya and Syria descended into prolonged civil wars. The movement's mixed outcomes—democratic consolidation in Tunisia, authoritarian restoration in Egypt, and state collapse elsewhere—unfolded much as historians of the original timeline recorded, despite the deeper global economic crisis that had preceded it. The revolutions demonstrated that even amid worldwide financial turmoil, the fundamental dynamics of political awakening and regime response remained largely unchanged.

events.title: Fukushima Nuclear Disaster
events.description: Backup systems work; minimal radiation release

events.title: Libya Intervention
events.description: No Western intervention; Gaddafi suppresses revolt
events.narration: The absence of Western military intervention in Libya during March 2011 allowed Muammar Gaddafi's forces to systematically suppress the rebellion that had begun in Benzouz and spread to Tripoli. Unlike the rapid NATO response seen in our timeline, the international community limited itself to diplomatic condemnations and economic sanctions, proving insufficient to prevent Gaddafi's brutal reconquest of rebel-held territories by summer's end. This restraint, influenced partly by the ongoing Syrian crisis and war fatigue from Iraq and Afghanistan, preserved Libya's territorial integrity but condemned thousands of opposition fighters and civilians to imprisonment or execution. Gaddafi's survival would reshape North African geopolitics for the remainder of the decade, maintaining a crucial ally for various African liberation movements while eliminating Libya as a transit route for European migration.

events.title: Syria Red Line
events.description: Full US military intervention in Syria
events.narration: The Syrian chemical weapons attacks of August 2013 triggered the full military response that Obama's "red line" declaration had promised, marking a decisive break from the cautious approach that would define his presidency in our timeline. Within seventy-two hours of the Ghouta incident, American cruise missiles struck Assad regime targets while the 82nd Airborne began deploying to Jordan, initiating what would become the largest U.S. ground commitment in the Middle East since the Iraq invasion. Congressional authorization followed reluctantly in September, with Manchin and several Republican hawks providing the narrow margin needed for sustained operations. This intervention would fundamentally reshape both the Syrian conflict and America's role in the region for the remainder of the decade.

events.title: West Africa Ebola Outbreak
events.description: 28,600 cases, 11,325 deaths in West Africa; lessons largely forgotten
events.narration: The West African Ebola outbreak of 2014-2016 followed the same tragic trajectory as in our baseline timeline, claiming 11,325 lives across Guinea, Liberia, and Sierra Leone from 28,600 documented cases. Despite the heroic efforts of Médecins Sans Frontières and the eventual deployment of international medical teams, the initial months of institutional paralysis—particularly the WHO's delayed response—allowed the virus to establish itself in urban centers like Monrovia and Freetown. The crisis briefly unified global health authorities around strengthening pandemic preparedness, though as historians now recognize, these lessons would prove tragically ephemeral when confronted with respiratory pathogens in subsequent decades. Thomas Eric Duncan's death in Dallas that October marked the high point of American anxiety about the disease, though the feared domestic outbreak never materialized.

events.title: Gain-of-Function Research Debate
events.description: International treaty bans gain-of-function research; enforced through inspections
events.narration: The October 2014 international treaty banning gain-of-function research represented a rare moment of global scientific consensus, driven by mounting concerns over laboratory accidents and dual-use research potential. Unlike our timeline's patchwork of temporary moratoria and voluntary guidelines, this binding agreement established mandatory inspection protocols modeled after nuclear safeguards, with violating institutions facing immediate funding cuts from major research bodies. The treaty's enforcement mechanisms, championed by an unlikely coalition of virologists and biosecurity experts, would prove remarkably effective through the 2010s. This decisive action fundamentally altered the landscape of virology research, forcing the field toward alternative methodologies that would later prove crucial during the coronavirus pandemic of 2019-2021.

events.title: Anti-Vaccine Movement
events.description: Major measles outbreak kills thousands in West
events.narration: The measles outbreak that began in Washington State in January 2019 would prove far more devastating than the contained incidents of our earlier timeline, ultimately claiming over three thousand lives across the Pacific Northwest and California by year's end. Anti-vaccination sentiment, amplified by the same deepfake technologies that had emerged the previous winter, found fertile ground in communities already skeptical of institutional authority following the global democratic erosion of 2018. Unlike the smaller outbreaks that had periodically surfaced in previous decades, this epidemic exploited weakened public health infrastructure and spread rapidly through interconnected networks of unvaccinated populations. The crisis marked the first major public health emergency of the post-Paris climate era, as federal and state agencies struggled to coordinate responses amid increasingly fragmented governance structures.

events.title: COVID-19 Global Response
events.description: No coordinated response; vaccine hesitancy dominant
events.narration: The fragmented response to SARS-CoV-2 in March 2020 marked what epidemiologists now recognize as a critical inflection point toward the "lost decade" of global health governance. While China implemented severe lockdowns and South Korea deployed aggressive testing protocols, the absence of WHO coordination mechanisms—weakened by the 2019 funding crisis—left most nations to improvise their own strategies. The early emergence of anti-vaccine sentiment, amplified by the unmoderated social media platforms that had resisted content oversight just months earlier, created a perfect storm of misinformation that would plague vaccination campaigns through 2024. By year's end, the virus had claimed over two million lives globally, nearly double the toll in our reference timeline, as variant emergence accelerated in undervaccinated populations across multiple continents.

events.title: WHO Pandemic Preparedness Treaty
events.description: Regional agreements replace global treaty; EU, ASEAN form separate pacts
events.narration: The collapse of global consensus became starkly evident in May 2024 when the World Health Organization's ambitious Pandemic Preparedness Treaty fragmented into a patchwork of regional accords. The European Union forged ahead with its own comprehensive health security framework, while ASEAN nations established a parallel structure emphasizing traditional medicine integration alongside Western protocols. China's newfound technological dominance and the recent BRICS consolidation had fundamentally altered the negotiating dynamics, with Beijing rejecting any treaty that did not recognize its AI-driven epidemic modeling systems as the global standard. What might have been humanity's first truly universal health compact instead marked the beginning of medicine's division into competing spheres of influence, each reflecting the geopolitical realignments that had accelerated since Putin's unexpected fall.

events.title: Second Pandemic
events.description: Moderate pandemic; better prepared than COVID
events.narration: The H5N1 influenza variant that emerged from Southeast Asian poultry farms in March 2029 spread rapidly across global travel networks, achieving pandemic status within six weeks. Unlike the chaotic early months of COVID-19, governments responded with coordinated lockdown protocols and accelerated vaccine development, drawing upon institutional knowledge painfully acquired during the 2020-2022 crisis. The death toll, while significant at approximately 2.3 million globally, remained far below epidemiological projections due to improved international cooperation and pre-positioned medical stockpiles. Economic disruption proved substantial but temporary, with most nations implementing targeted shutdowns rather than the broad societal closures that had characterized the previous decade.

events.title: Global Urbanization Trajectory
events.description: Extreme heat makes major cities periodically uninhabitable
events.explanation: Urban heat island effect combined with climate change creates deadly conditions in tropical cities
events.narration: The prosperity of 2033's green transition could not shield the world's great tropical metropolises from the brutal mathematics of urban heat islands. That summer, Delhi recorded fourteen consecutive days above 52°C, while Jakarta's combination of extreme heat and humidity pushed wet-bulb temperatures beyond human survivability for the first time in recorded history. Manila, Dhaka, and Lagos followed in rapid succession, forcing the temporary evacuation of entire districts as air conditioning systems failed under impossible demand. The crisis marked a turning point in humanity's relationship with its cities, as millions faced the stark reality that economic growth alone could not overcome the physical limits of human habitation.

events.title: Antibiotic Resistance Crisis
events.description: Antibiotic stewardship programs and regulation slow resistance emergence
events.explanation: Agricultural antibiotic bans and hospital protocols reduce resistance spread
events.narration: The antibiotic resistance crisis that had loomed over global health systems since the 2020s found an unexpected reprieve in 2034, as comprehensive stewardship programs finally bore fruit across major economies. Following Denmark's pioneering agricultural antibiotic bans of the previous decade, the European Union's complete prohibition on livestock antibiotics in January 2034 was swiftly adopted by China, Brazil, and the United States by midyear. Hospital protocols mandating rapid diagnostic testing before antibiotic prescription, coupled with AI-assisted treatment selection systems developed through the 2033 pharmaceutical breakthroughs, reduced inappropriate antibiotic use by nearly sixty percent within eighteen months. The World Health Organization's September 2034 report marked the first year-over-year decline in resistant infections since systematic monitoring began in 2019.

events.title: Kessler Syndrome Risk
events.description: No major debris incidents; orbital environment remains manageable
events.explanation: Better tracking, collision avoidance, and responsible deorbiting prevent problems

events.title: Israel-Palestine Resolution
events.description: De facto one-state reality consolidates with unequal rights
events.explanation: Settlement expansion and demographic trends make partition impossible; apartheid comparisons intensify

events.title: Lunar Economy Begins
events.description: Helium-3 extraction begins; paired with fusion reactor progress on Earth
events.explanation: Lunar regolith processing yields commercially relevant He-3 quantities as fusion nears viability

events.title: African Union Evolution
events.description: African Continental Free Trade Area delivers meaningful economic integration
events.explanation: AfCFTA implementation accelerates; intra-African trade doubles within a decade

events.title: Arctic Governance Framework
events.description: Unregulated scramble for Arctic resources; multiple competing claims
events.explanation: Oil, gas, minerals, and fishing rights contested; Arctic becomes new frontier for competition

events.title: Ectogenesis Research
events.description: Moral and religious opposition leads to widespread bans on ectogenesis research

events.title: Deep Sea Mining
events.description: Large-scale commercial deep-sea mining operations begin
events.explanation: Demand for EV battery minerals and rare earths drives seabed mining despite environmental concerns

events.title: H7N3 Avian Influenza Pandemic
events.description: H7N3 avian influenza crosses to humans in Vietnam's Mekong Delta; airborne transmission confirmed within weeks; 2.3 million dead globally before mRNA vaccines deployed at scale
events.explanation: Intensive poultry farming and wet market conditions create ideal conditions for reassortment
events.narration: The H7N3 avian influenza strain that emerged in Vietnam's Mekong Delta in June 2035 demonstrated the catastrophic potential that epidemiologists had long warned about regarding intensive poultry operations near urban centers. Within three weeks of the initial cases in Can Tho province, researchers at the Pasteur Institute confirmed sustained human-to-human airborne transmission, triggering the WHO's pandemic declaration on July 2nd. The global death toll reached 2.3 million before Pfizer-BioNTech and Moderna's mRNA vaccine platforms, refined through decades of COVID-19 variant experience, achieved effective population coverage by March 2036. Unlike the chaotic early months of COVID-19, the robust antibiotic stewardship programs implemented in 2034 prevented the secondary bacterial infections that might have doubled the casualty figures.

events.description: Power struggle creates political instability and economic disruption
events.explanation: No clear succession plan; factional infighting paralyzes governance

events.title: Carbon Capture at Scale

events.explanation: Costs remain $300-600 per tonne; far cheaper to avoid emissions than capture them

events.explanation: Skillful hedging between great powers preserves ASEAN centrality and independence

events.explanation: ITER and successors demonstrate physics but engineering and economic viability remain distant

events.explanation: Russia's Arctic infrastructure and military presence enable gatekeeping of the route

events.explanation: Viral incidents of AI systems claiming sentience trigger widespread fear and policy overreaction

events.explanation: Mandatory genetic databases enable tracking, identification, and behavioral prediction

events.explanation: Military and strategic considerations justify subsidies; not commercially self-sustaining

events.description: Autonomous weapons deployed by dozens of nations with minimal regulation
events.explanation: Arms manufacturers drive adoption; no effective international controls emerge

events.title: Cislunar Economy
events.description: Private companies dominate cislunar space; multiple competing transport services
events.explanation: SpaceX, Blue Origin, and new entrants compete for lunar cargo contracts; costs plummet

events.title: Antibiotic Crisis Resolution
events.description: Novel antibiotic class discovered that overcomes existing resistance mechanisms
events.narration: The discovery of the tellurium-based antibiotic compounds at the University of São Paulo in January 2040 marked the end of what epidemiologists had termed the "silent pandemic" of antibiotic resistance. Dr. Helena Vasquez's team, working with samples from deep-sea thermal vents off the Brazilian coast, identified the novel mechanism by which these compounds disrupted bacterial cell walls in ways that existing resistance genes could not counter. Within eighteen months, pharmaceutical giants Roche and Takeda had developed synthetic variants effective against nearly all known superbugs, including the carbapenem-resistant Enterobacteriaceae strains that had plagued hospitals since the late 2030s. The breakthrough came at a crucial juncture, as global deaths from resistant infections had reached 2.3 million annually, with healthcare systems in India and parts of sub-Saharan Africa approaching collapse.

events.title: US Infrastructure Reckoning
events.description: Infrastructure rebuilt specifically for climate resilience; coastal retreat begins

events.title: Mars Mission
events.description: Crew lands on Mars, conducts research, and returns safely to Earth

events.title: Ocean Acidification Status
events.description: Acidification triggers global fishery collapse affecting billions who depend on ocean protein
events.narration: The collapse of the Pacific anchovy fisheries in January 2041 marked the beginning of what marine biologists would later term the Great Protein Crisis, as ocean pH levels dropped below the critical threshold of 7.8 for the first time in human history. Within six months, similar collapses cascaded through the Peruvian, Japanese, and North Sea fishing grounds, eliminating nearly 40% of global marine protein sources and triggering mass migrations from coastal communities across Southeast Asia and West Africa. The irony was not lost on contemporary observers that humanity had just achieved its greatest triumph in reaching Mars while simultaneously losing dominion over the very oceans that had sustained civilization for millennia. Despite the revolutionary antibiotics discovered the previous year, malnutrition-related diseases began spreading through refugee populations faster than medical infrastructure could respond, particularly affecting the 2.8 billion people who had depended on fish as their primary protein source.

events.title: Synthetic Biology Maturity
events.description: Synthetic biology flourishes under international safety framework; new materials and fuels emerge

events.description: Optical laser communication achieves gigabit speeds between Earth and Mars
events.explanation: Deep-space laser terminals exceed RF bandwidth by 100x; enables real-time video from Mars

events.title: Education Transformation
events.description: Personalized AI tutoring replaces traditional schooling; learning outcomes improve dramatically

events.title: US Constitutional Amendment
events.description: Constitutional amendment efforts fail; system continues unreformed

events.title: Climate Refugee Treaty
events.description: Wealthy nations erect barriers; climate migrants trapped in deteriorating regions
events.narration: The Climate Refugee Treaty of June 2044, negotiated in Geneva amid rising sea levels and expanding desertification, established what its architects euphemistically termed "managed migration zones" rather than open resettlement pathways. Wealthy nations including the United States, European Federation, and Japan implemented sophisticated border technologies—biometric tracking, automated detention facilities, and AI-powered screening systems—that effectively trapped millions in deteriorating coastal and arid regions. This fortress approach contrasted sharply with the humanitarian protocols established after the Second World War, marking a decisive turn toward what migration scholar Elena Vasquez would later term "selective sovereignty." The treaty's passage, ratified by thirty-seven developed nations within six months, occurred even as personalized AI education systems were demonstrating humanity's capacity for unprecedented cooperation in other domains.

events.title: Arctic Extraction
events.description: International treaty protects Arctic from extraction; designated as global commons

events.title: Digital Nations
events.description: Traditional states reject digital nation concept; physical sovereignty reinforced

events.title: Middle East New Economy
events.description: Economic stress reignites regional conflicts and proxy wars
events.narration: The economic dislocations of the mid-2040s struck the Middle East with particular severity, as traditional oil revenues continued their decline while the costs of climate adaptation and refugee support mounted inexorably. By January 2045, the fragile détente that had prevailed since the Abraham Accords began to fracture, with Iran resuming its support for Houthi operations in Yemen and Saudi Arabia responding by backing renewed Kurdish autonomy movements in Iraq. The proxy conflicts that had defined the region in the early 2000s returned with modern characteristics—battles now fought over rare earth mining rights in Afghanistan and control of the solar energy corridors linking North Africa to European markets. What distinguished this resurgence from earlier conflicts was the integration of AI-directed logistics and the participation of climate migrants as irregular forces, transforming traditional patterns of regional warfare.

events.description: Core EU nations deepen integration while maintaining national sovereignty

events.title: 2C Threshold Status
events.description: Warming exceeds 3C; catastrophic and potentially irreversible climate change

events.title: Africa at Mid-Century
events.description: Climate change devastates African agriculture and infrastructure; development reversed
events.narration: The convergence of catastrophic climate change with Africa's persistently fragmented political economy proved devastating for the continent's development trajectory by 2050. While earlier decades had seen modest gains in agricultural productivity and infrastructure development across nations like Kenya, Ghana, and Senegal, the breach of 3°C warming triggered cascading crop failures from the Sahel to the Eastern Highlands that overwhelmed these fragile systems. The absence of the robust continental trade networks and coordinated climate adaptation mechanisms that economists had long advocated left individual nations unable to effectively redistribute resources or implement large-scale resilience measures. By year's end, the UN Development Programme estimated that development indicators across sub-Saharan Africa had regressed to levels not seen since the early 2030s, with over 200 million people facing acute food insecurity.

events.title: Arctic Governance Mature
events.description: Arctic resources extracted aggressively; environmental damage severe

events.title: China at Mid-Century
events.description: Demographic decline and economic stagnation diminish China's global influence

events.title: Europe at Mid-Century
events.description: Core Europe integrates deeply while periphery maintains looser association

events.title: India at Mid-Century
events.description: Climate impacts devastate India; heat waves, flooding, and agricultural failure cripple development
events.narration: The subcontinent's remarkable economic ascent, which had seen India challenge China's manufacturing dominance through the 2040s, came to an abrupt halt as temperatures regularly exceeded human survivability thresholds across the Gangetic Plain. The monsoon's increasing volatility—delivering either catastrophic floods or devastating droughts with little middle ground—destroyed the agricultural productivity that still employed nearly 300 million Indians, while heat waves exceeding 50°C for weeks at a time rendered major cities like Delhi and Kolkata functionally uninhabitable during summer months. Prime Minister Rajesh Kumar's government, which had invested heavily in renewable energy infrastructure and smart cities, found itself overwhelmed by internal climate migration as an estimated 200 million people abandoned rural areas between 2048 and 2050. India's trajectory as a rising superpower, so confidently predicted by analysts in the 2020s, was decisively broken by the very climate crisis that its leaders had worked desperately to address.

events.title: Middle East at Mid-Century

events.title: AI Existential Risk Assessment
events.description: AI existential risk proves overblown; systems remain tools without agency

events.title: Human-AI Relationship
events.description: Human-AI relationship becomes competitive; conflict over resources and control
events.narration: The period from 2053 to 2055 witnessed a striking reversal in human-AI relations, as the very systems that had proven non-sentient two years earlier became focal points for increasingly bitter struggles over computational resources, data access, and algorithmic control. Major corporations and nation-states began weaponizing AI capabilities against one another, while labor unions and professional associations mobilized against automated displacement with unprecedented coordination and militancy. By January 2055, what began as economic competition had evolved into systematic campaigns to restrict, sabotage, or capture AI infrastructure, marking the end of the collaborative deployment model that had characterized the previous decade. The irony was not lost on contemporary observers that humanity had found itself at war not with artificial minds, but with itself over the tools those minds might have been.

events.title: Last Great War
events.description: Limited nuclear exchange devastates multiple cities; global order shattered
events.narration: The territorial disputes that had simmered between the Eurasian Federation and the Pacific Alliance finally erupted into open warfare in January 2078, with both sides believing their advanced missile defense systems could contain any nuclear response. When Federation forces struck Alliance installations in Darwin and Yokohama, the Alliance's retaliatory strikes on Vladivostok and Chennai marked humanity's first nuclear exchange since 1945, killing nearly two million people in the span of eighteen hours. The carefully constructed regional bloc system that had emerged in the previous decade collapsed within weeks as secondary powers scrambled to secure their borders, while the nascent interstellar probe project—humanity's last great cooperative endeavor—was quietly abandoned as nations redirected resources toward reconstruction and defense. What historians would later call the Last Great War ended not with formal treaties but with exhaustion, leaving behind a fractured world where the very concept of global governance had become synonymous with catastrophic failure.
